---
title: "Session 11 teaching notes: Python 8"
session_id: session-11
event_id: null
---

## Session 11 — Pandas automation and web APIs

Note: Klausur: Pandas-Teil ist "Pure pandas" (keine Kombination mit Python)

Alpha Vantage keys: PR shares them later.

JSON: relevance (apps, ...)

API examples: depending on remaining time.

See `5_REST.py`


Keep API examples non-executable in rendered notes and select them according to the remaining class time.

## Exercises

| Slide(s) | Exercise                    | Summary                                                                                                                                   | Files                       | In-class / take-home |
| ------: | --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | -------------------- |
|     143 | Stock-market API            | Retrieve Mercedes-Benz data through an API, plot closing prices, and calculate and visualize high/low returns.                            | `MercedesBenz1.py`          |                      |
|     148 | Stock-price collection      | Retrieve evening closing prices for four stocks and append them with the date to a persistent CSV file.                                   | `Stocks.py`<br>`Stocks.csv` |                      |
|     152 | JSON data retrieval         | Rewrite the Mercedes-Benz exercise to process the API response as JSON.                                                                   | `MercedesBenz2.py`          |                      |
|     155 | Cryptocurrency API analysis | Retrieve Bitcoin and Ethereum prices, convert timestamps, calculate returns, variances, and correlation, and visualize the return series. | `Cryptocurrencies.py`       |                      |
|     159 | Weather API                 | Configure an Open-Meteo forecast, retrieve it through the API, and process the resulting weather data.                                    | —                           |                      |

## Slide 137 — Calculate a moving average with a loop

The loop calculates the mean of the preceding ten observations and writes it into the `MA10` column. The first ten positions remain empty because no complete ten-observation window is available.

```python
import pandas as pd

data = pd.read_csv('Stockprice.csv')
data.head()

data['MA10'] = None
n = 10

for i in range(n, len(data)):
    data.iloc[i, 2] = data.iloc[(i - n):i, 1].mean()

data.head()
data.head(15)

data.plot(
    y=['price', 'MA10'],
    use_index=True,
)
```

## Slide 138 — Define a semivariance function

Semivariance includes only observations below the mean. While migrating this fragment, the condition was corrected to compare the observed value (rather than its row position) with the average, and the accumulator was renamed so it does not shadow Python's built-in `sum()`.

```python
def semivariance(values):
    average = values.mean()
    squared_deviation_sum = 0
    count = 0

    for i in range(len(values)):
        if values.iloc[i] < average:
            squared_deviation_sum += (
                values.iloc[i] - average
            ) ** 2
            count += 1

    semivar = squared_deviation_sum / count
    return semivar


semivariance(data['price'])
```

## General teaching notes

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class demo]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-demo} [`../materials/session_11/5_REST.py`](../materials/session_11/5_REST.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-demo}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_11/5_REST.py >}}
```

- Explain why JSON is relevant to applications.
- Choose API examples according to the remaining time.
- Alpha Vantage keys will be shared later by PR.

::: {.callout-warning title="API execution"}
Do not execute API scripts while rendering these notes. They may require network access, API keys, or current external responses.
:::

::: {.callout-important title="Exam relevance"}
The pandas portion of the exam is “pure pandas”; it does not combine pandas with the Python topics taught here.
:::

## Slide 143 — Stock-market API

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_11/MercedesBenz1.py`](../materials/session_11/MercedesBenz1.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_11/MercedesBenz1.py >}}
```

## Slide 148 — Stock-price collection

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_11/Stocks.py`](../materials/session_11/Stocks.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_11/Stocks.py >}}
```

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/data/Stocks.csv`](../materials/data/Stocks.csv){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

::: {.teaching-break}
☕ Break — 10 minutes
:::

## Slide 152 — JSON data retrieval

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_11/MercedesBenz2.py`](../materials/session_11/MercedesBenz2.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_11/MercedesBenz2.py >}}
```

## Slide 155 — Cryptocurrency API analysis

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/session_11/Cryptocurrencies.py`](../materials/session_11/Cryptocurrencies.py){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise}
:::

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
{{< include ../materials/session_11/Cryptocurrencies.py >}}
```

## Slide 159 — Weather API

- Configure and process the weather API example described in the exercise table.
- No source filename is provided in the existing notes or material inventory.

## Summary and announcements

- TODO

::: {.callout-tip title="Notes for improvement"}
Take notes on improvements and common questions during the session and add them to `feedback.qmd` afterwards.
:::
